criteria.title: Purpose and guiding principles
probes: Ordinance states the purpose or legislative intent for outdoor lighting rules.
Dark skies, night sky preservation, or astronomy is named as a goal.
Human health or circadian impacts from light at night are addressed.
Wildlife, ecology, or nocturnal ecosystems are mentioned.
Energy use, emissions, or efficiency of outdoor lighting is a stated aim.
Tourism related to dark skies, stars, or astrotourism appears.

criteria.title: Scope of regulated lighting
probes: Which outdoor fixtures or lamps the ordinance regulates.
Permanent and portable outdoor artificial lighting are in scope.
New versus existing installations or post-effective-date applicability.
Flood, general illumination, signage, or advertisement lighting covered.

criteria.title: Shielding and downward control
probes: Full shielding, full cutoff, or downcast fixture requirements.
Outdoor light must be confined to the property of origin.
BUG rating or IES lighting standards referenced.
Shielding or cutoff varies by lighting class or lumen output.

criteria.title: Light trespass and glare at boundaries
probes: Light trespass, spill, or glare limits at property lines.
Foot-candle or illuminance limits at boundaries.
A person off-site must not see the lamp or bulb directly.
Direct illumination must not shine on adjacent properties.

criteria.title: Spectral quality and color temperature
probes: Correlated color temperature or Kelvin maximum for outdoor sources.
Warm white, amber, or yellow light preferred over blue-rich white.
Different spectral rules by lighting class where color rendering matters.
Circadian, skyglow, or ecological harms of high-CCT white mentioned.

criteria.title: Uplighting and upward-directed light
probes: Uplighting or upward-directed architectural light is restricted or prohibited.
Landscape uplight limited to low-level or grade-mounted sources if allowed.
Up-light allowed only under eaves or dense vegetation when an exception exists.

criteria.title: After-hours lighting and string lights
probes: Commercial or business outdoor lights must dim or shut off after late evening.
Curfew tied to 11 p.m. or close of business for some outdoor lighting.
Residential string lights or decorative sources have timing or shutoff rules.
Security lighting carve-outs while still limiting nuisance or trespass.

criteria.title: Controls, motion sensing, and smart lighting
probes: Motion sensors, timers, or dimmers are required or encouraged for outdoor light.
Short shutoff delay after motion activation.
Shielding or trespass rules tied to motion-activated fixtures.
Principle that light should occur only when needed and dim or turn off when not.

criteria.title: Site-wide and per-fixture lumen budgets
probes: Total outdoor lumens or brightness capped by land use or acreage.
Per-fixture lumen maximums for outdoor lighting.
Different lumen budgets for single-family, multi-family, or non-residential sites.

criteria.title: Lighting classes by use (canopy, security, decorative)
probes: Lighting classes such as canopy, security, or decorative with different rules.
Class 1, Class 2, Class 3 style distinctions or equivalent use-based tiers.
Spectral, shielding, or timing rules differ by class or use type.

criteria.title: Public street lighting
probes: Municipal or utility street lighting is addressed.
LED street replacement, shielding, uplight, or glare limits.
BUG rating or similar street luminaire performance criteria.

criteria.title: Greenhouse and intensive agricultural lighting
probes: Greenhouse, grow-facility, or intensive horticultural lighting is regulated.
Blackout screens, curtains, or facade limits on visible grow light.
Curfews or shielding so light does not leave the site.

criteria.title: Signage: internal, external, and digital displays
probes: Internally lit signs: luminance, hours off, or size limits.
Externally lit signs: aiming, mounting from top, or spotlight constraints.
Electronic or digital displays: brightness, timing, or billboard-specific rules.
Sign lighting must go off after sunset, business close, or overnight curfew.

criteria.title: Parking, display lots, and decorative outdoor lighting
probes: Parking lot outdoor lighting: shielding, trespass, or brightness limits.
Vehicle display lots or car dealerships have outdoor lighting rules.
Decorative or architectural landscape lighting paired with curfew or class rules.

criteria.title: Holiday and sports or recreational lighting
probes: Seasonal holiday decorative lighting: allowed dates or maximum duration.
Sports or large recreational fields: curfew, uplight fraction, or intensity caps.
IES or DarkSky sports lighting guidance referenced for fields.

criteria.title: Plans, permits, and design review
probes: Site plan or building permit requires a lighting fixture schedule or cut sheets.
Photometrics, lumen totals, mounting heights, or CCT submitted for review.
Staff verifies outdoor lighting conforms before permit approval.

criteria.title: Inspection, enforcement, timelines, and exemptions
probes: Field inspection of installed lighting against approved plans.
Complaint-based enforcement, civil infractions, nuisance, or penalties described.
Effective dates, grandfathering, or upgrade timelines for existing fixtures.
Exemptions listed such as airport, construction, infrared, or temporary lighting.
